title: feat: load What's New dynamically from news branch
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Fresh news, straight from the hive
gist.blurb: The 'What's New' section on our homepage now updates dynamically! No more waiting for a site deployment to see the latest buzz, plus clickable links and a shiny new roadmap scoring system.
gist.summary: The frontend now dynamically fetches the latest news highlights directly from the repository, eliminating the need for manual hardcoded updates.
gist.impact: Users will see real-time updates in the 'What's New' section with fully clickable markdown links, alongside a newly scored 'What's Next' roadmap.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about dynamic news delivery and updating a display. Characters: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot. Polly could be handing a fresh newspaper or a glowing data scroll to the Monitor Robot, whose screen displays a news feed or roadmap.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny newsboy cap, handing a glowing digital newspaper to a chunky Monitor Robot. The robot's CRT screen displays a neat, colorful news feed. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
